Title: Alteration of S-adenosylhomocysteine levels affects lignin biosynthesis in switchgrass

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCOMT activity was directly and significantly inhibited by SAH in crude extract assays. 2. PvSAHH1 expression positively correlated with PvCOMT in wild-type switchgrass. 3. PvCOMT is a methyltransferase whose activity helps determine S and G lignin biosynthesis under altered SAH/SAM balance, but it was not genetically manipulated as the focal gene.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with PvSAHH1 expression in wild-type switchgrass
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support and reduced digestibility when abundant
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enzymatic activity is inhibited by SAH, linking SAH accumulation to reduced monolignol methylation and lower lignin deposition
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crude enzyme activity assay with different SAM/SAH ratios; expression correlation analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays using wild-type stem extracts from E4 stage
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports manipulating upstream SAH homeostasis rather than only overexpressing COMT because substrate/inhibitor balance limits methylation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Prior literature cited in paper indicates COMT overexpression alone does not necessarily increase lignin accumulation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the paper experimentally validated SAH inhibition of its activity, though it is not one of the main genetically manipulated core genes

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCCoAOMT activity was directly and significantly inhibited by SAH in crude extract assays. 2. PvSAHH1 expression positively correlated with PvCCoAOMT in wild-type switchgrass. 3. PvCCoAOMT participates in monolignol methylation whose efficiency depends strongly on SAH/SAM ratio.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with PvSAHH1 expression in wild-type switchgrass
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural support and reduced digestibility when abundant
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enzyme activity is suppressed by SAH, providing biochemical support that elevated SAH lowers lignin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Crude enzyme activity assay with varied SAM/SAH ratios; expression correlation analysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro enzyme assays using wild-type stem extracts from E4 stage
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Indicates that modulating methyl-donor/inhibitor homeostasis can control endogenous CCoAOMT activity and lignin output
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally assayed but not genetically manipulated as a focal gene

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvCGS encodes cystathionine gamma-synthase in methionine metabolism and was the first monocot CGS functionally studied here. 2. RNAi down-regulation of PvCGS reduced SAH by 38.1%–40.8% and increased SAM/SAH ratios. 3. PvCGS down-regulation increased both G and S lignin monomer yields and caused severe growth stunting and delayed flowering. 4. PvCGS down-regulation also elevated Asp-derived amino acids and aromatic amino acids including phenylalanine and tyrosine, which may further support lignin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Positively correlated with PvSAMS and PvSAHH1 during internode lignification; examined in internodes I2-I4, leaf sheaths, and leaf blades across E2, E3, E4, and R1 stages
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High/induced during internode lignification; stems at E4 used for analysis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: well-lignified stem internode tissues including fibres, sclerenchyma, and vascular bundles
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cell wall recalcitrance and structural support; reduction improves cell wall digestibility/saccharification
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool and modulates methyl-donor balance for monolignol methylation by altering SAH and SAM/SAH ratio
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: methionine biosynthesis / C1 metabolism linked to lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: aspartate-family amino acid pathway feeding methionine cycle and methyl-donor metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses O-phosphohomoserine with cysteine to form cystathionine in the plant methionine biosynthetic pathway
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Rate-limiting and committed enzyme in methionine biosynthesis as stated in Discussion
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulation decreases SAH and increases SAM/SAH ratio, thereby enhancing G and S lignin accumulation; also causes severe dwarfing and delayed flowering
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in switchgrass; qRT-PCR; ELISA measurement of SAM/SAH/Hcy; LC-MS/MS amino acid profiling; phloroglucinol-HCl staining; thioacidolysis-GC/MS lignin analysis; RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse, 26 C, 16-h light; stems/internodes sampled mainly at E4 stage
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (no accession reported); amino acid profiling by LC-MS/MS
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: No direct epigenetic regulation shown; transcriptome results were interpreted as excluding major SAM-mediated DNA methylation effects on lignin-pathway and Met-cycling gene expression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper reports little change in free methionine despite strong perturbation of methionine-cycle intermediates and major increases in Asp-derived amino acids; enhanced lignin may also be supported by elevated phenylalanine and tyrosine
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to alter lignin via methionine metabolism, but strong growth penalty makes PvCGS down-regulation less suitable alone for biomass crop improvement
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Severe growth stunting, shorter internodes, reduced plant height, delayed/no flowering under tested conditions

Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PvSAHH1 encodes S-adenosylhomocysteine hydrolase and is highly expressed in well-lignified tissues. 2. RNAi down-regulation of PvSAHH1 increased SAH by 59%–96% and decreased SAM/SAH ratios. 3. PvSAHH1 down-regulation reduced both G and S lignin accumulation and increased cell wall saccharification efficiency by 22%–85%. 4. Selected RNAi lines showed normal morphology, making PvSAHH1 a promising engineering target.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in well-lignified tissues; positively correlated with PvCOMT and PvCCoAOMT expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: High in stems/well-lignified tissues; analyzed mainly at E4 stage
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: stem internodes/cell walls of well-lignified tissues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides cell wall strength and recalcitrance; reducing lignin improves biomass digestibility
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required to remove inhibitory SAH and sustain methylation flux toward target product
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: methionine cycle / activated methyl cycle linked to lignin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: SAH recycling step controlling methyl-donor homeostasis for monolignol methylation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes S-adenosylhomocysteine to homocysteine and adenosine, relieving SAH-mediated inhibition of methyltransferases
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for SAH homeostasis affecting monolignol methylation; explicit rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Down-regulation raises SAH and lowers SAM/SAH ratio, reducing G and S lignin accumulation and improving saccharification efficiency without obvious abnormal morphology in selected lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi knockdown in switchgrass; qRT-PCR; ELISA for SAM/SAH/Hcy; thioacidolysis-GC/MS lignin analysis; saccharification assay
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Glasshouse, 26 C, 16-h light; stems sampled at E4 stage
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A second homolog, PvSAHH2 (Pavir.6KG019200.1), contains a 133-bp N-terminal deletion causing a frameshift and was not pursued as the functional target
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising cell wall bioengineering target to reduce lignin and increase digestibility with lower visible growth penalty than PvCGS knockdown
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential off-target effects on other SAM-dependent methylation processes were not deeply characterized; selected lines appeared morphologically normal
